top_signals.title: ‘Soft print, hard regime’: Bitcoin climbs toward $64,000 as June CPI falls 0.4% in largest monthly drop since 2020
top_signals.summary: June CPI fell 0.4% and core held at 2.6% annually, lifting BTC toward $64,000 as analysts flip from fears to a summer recovery case.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Pares Gains as Trump Backs Off 20% Fee for Hormuz Shipments
top_signals.summary: Oil swung as US President Donald Trump backed away from his plan to demand payment for cargo moving through the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: U.S. Gasoline Prices Rise for First Time Since May as Iran Ceasefire Collapses
top_signals.summary: U.S. gasoline prices have increased for the first time since May, with the sudden reversal directly tied to the collapse of the tentative ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. According to AAA data, the U.S. national average price of gasoline clocked in at $3.8590 per gallon on Tuesday, up from $3.7900 a week ago after the end of the ceasefire sparked renewed military exchanges and disruptions in the critical Strait of Hormuz. According to data compiled by GasBuddy, nearly 80% of states saw retail price increases, mirroring a similar rally in crude…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
top_signals.summary: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade
top_signals.summary: Some Chinese refiners have not nominated term crude cargoes from Saudi Arabia for August, while others have not been allocated any term supply for next month, as weak demand in China, competition from other producers, and continued disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz have slashed Saudi supply in recent months. At least two refiners in China have not asked for any term cargo deliveries for August, and a few others have not received provisional allocation volumes, traders with direct knowledge of the supply arrangements told Bloomberg on Tuesday.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Big Rig Crash Closes Eastbound I-80 Near Donner Pass
top_signals.summary:
A big rig crash closed eastbound Interstate 80 near Donner Pass early Tuesday morning, causing major traffic delays in California’s Sierra Nevada. According to CBS News, the crash happened shortly after 3:30 a.m. near the Donner Pass Commercial Vehicle Enforcement Facility, east of Truckee. According to reporting from CBS News Sacramento, the crash happened shortly […]
